text.title: FinOps INFORM Phase — Cost Visibility Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: All taggable resources MUST carry the mandatory tag set: `team`, `product`, `environment`, `cost-center`, `owner`. Untagged resources trigger a daily compliance report.
rule.rationale: Without these five tags chargeback is impossible; every other FinOps practice depends on this.

rule.statement: Tag policy MUST be declared as code (OPA, AWS Config, Azure Policy) and enforced at provisioning time. Manual tagging post-hoc is rejected.
rule.rationale: Manual tagging decays; preventive enforcement catches violations before they become spend.

rule.statement: A persistent cost dashboard MUST exist with at minimum: total spend, spend by team, spend by environment, spend by service. Refresh ≤24h.

rationale.source: Dashboards as the org's shared truth

rule.rationale: Verbal cost discussions diverge — a shared dashboard is the only stable artefact.

rule.statement: KPI baselines MUST be published: $/active-user, $/revenue, $/MAU (or domain equivalent). Without baselines, ROI of subsequent optimization is unmeasurable.
rule.rationale: Saving 20% on spend means nothing if revenue dropped 30%; unit metrics anchor the conversation.

rule.statement: Tag coverage ≥80% is a hard gate for proceeding to OPTIMIZE phase; <80% = stay in INFORM and fix tagging first.
rule.rationale: Optimizing on noisy data produces wrong decisions; INFORM gates protect downstream work.

rule.statement: Skip when (a) tag coverage ≥95% AND dashboards exist AND chargeback live; OR (b) single-team single-product environment where attribution overhead exceeds value.
rule.rationale: If INFORM is already done, time is better spent on OPTIMIZE; below-threshold orgs don't need the ceremony.
